purpose: Header for IconMenu, the central app component. Owns the AudioDeviceManager, AudioPluginFormatManager, two KnownPluginLists (known vs active), the realtime AudioProcessorGraph, the AudioProcessorPlayer driving it, and the PreferencesWindow lifetime. Subclasses SystemTrayIconComponent + Timer + ChangeListener so it can render the tray icon, run periodic UI refresh, and listen for KnownPluginList changes.
invariants: Menu action ID offsets (kEditOffset=1M, kBypassOffset=2M, kDeleteOffset=3M, kMoveUpOffset=4M, kMoveDownOffset=5M) partition the popup-menu ID space into five non-overlapping 1M-entry ranges. Up to 1M plugins per action category — practically unbounded.
Forward-declares PreferencesWindow rather than including PreferencesWindow.h to break a header cycle; the .cpp file pulls the full type when constructing the window.
PluginLoadThread is a private nested class, forward-declared and unique_ptr-owned so the public header stays free of std::thread/JUCE Thread includes.

pluginLoadGeneration is std::atomic<int> (v4.0.3). The counter cancels in-flight background plugin loads when the user reloads or changes the chain. IconMenu's reads/writes happen on the message thread, but PluginLoadThread captures the value at start; the atomic type makes the cross-thread semantics explicit and guards future callers.
JUCE_DECLARE_NON_COPYABLE_WITH_LEAK_DETECTOR — IconMenu is single-instance, owned by PluginHostApp, never copied.
Windows-only members getStartupShortcutPath / isStartupEnabled / setStartupEnabled are guarded by JUCE_WINDOWS — not present on macOS/Linux ABI.
gotchas: sortedPluginCache + sortedCacheDirty are 'mutable' so const accessors can lazily rebuild the cache. Any code that mutates the active plugin list MUST set sortedCacheDirty=true or callers will see stale ordering.
isHandlingDeviceChange guards against re-entry in changeListenerCallback during the device-type / device-setup mutation paths; do not bypass it.
preferencesWindow is a unique_ptr — calling showPreferences while one already exists must check for null and reuse rather than reconstruct.
inputNodeId / outputNodeId are the graph's audio I/O processor node IDs, established once at construction. Don't rebuild them on every reconnect; reconnectGraph relies on their stability.
